chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Next we will talk about JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which is full form is JavaScript extension syntax

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: This is a syntax which is used to combine JavaScript with HTML

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So it lets us write HTML directly inside JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: In this, this JSX syntax

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: cannot be converted directly into JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It is not like you will write JSX in a JavaScript file

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: in a normal project

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: then our code will run

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So JSX is not real JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It is not real JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: The syntax we have learnt in JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: does not follow all the syntax here

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: But what JSX does is

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: whenever JSX is written in the React form

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: it internally converts to JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It is transpiled to that code

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: And that code has a special tool which transpiles

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which is called Babel

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Babel is a tool which transpiles JSX code

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which converts it to pure JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So whenever we write JSX code in React

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: First it is in JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Then it is used as an internal tool

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which converts it to JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: And finally the rendering on the page

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: or the logic of our page

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: or whatever things are displayed on our browser

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: All these are displayed using this JavaScript code

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Now in this, the beginner can hit the first question in our mind

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: that when we had to display JavaScript on a browser

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So why did JSX write code

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which Babel has transpiled to

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and then converts it to JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: The simplest answer is

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: that if we learn JSX once

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: we will cover JSX in one or two classes

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So it is very easy to implement JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: When we start writing component-wise things

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: it becomes more readable

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and it becomes easier to write

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: as compared to normal JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: to render the same amount of UI components

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: But the whole conversion of JSX to JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: internally which Babel does

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: is behind the scenes

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: It will never show us

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: that it will react internally

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: when we set up our computer

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So our internal tools will make this conversion

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We will not have to write any code separately

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We will not have to do anything separately

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: If we want we can also go to the Babel page

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which is Babel.js.io

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: If we go here

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and click on try it out

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: then we will open some code like this

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Now here in the sandbox

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which we have seen the code of the app

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We will copy it

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and paste it here

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So this was our code of the React

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which we had written in JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Babel internally changes it

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: in this format

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and this format is our pure JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: All the things in this will show you

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: JavaScript, Function, My Button

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: What is returning

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Some call is being made in the name of JSX

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Button is passing through as a string

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Object is passing through

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: In which children are written

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Again we have a function

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: in the name of My App

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: JSXS is returning

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: a div is being created

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: H1 element is being created in the children of div

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So this is how Babel works

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Babel is the pure JSX code

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: which converts it into a normal JavaScript

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: in some way

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: But Babel will automatically do this conversion

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We don't need to partake in it

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: So this was the basic of React

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: How does React work

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and in React it is written in the code format

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: Now we are finally going to set up

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: the React on our local machine

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: We will start creating a React project

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: a basic app

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: and with that we will slowly understand

chunks.title: What is JSX_.mp3
chunks.text: how we can build our components

text: Next we will talk about JSX which is full form is JavaScript extension syntax This is a syntax which is used to combine JavaScript with HTML So it lets us write HTML directly inside JavaScript In this, this JSX syntax cannot be converted directly into JavaScript It is not like you will write JSX in a JavaScript file in a normal project then our code will run So JSX is not real JavaScript It is not real JavaScript The syntax we have learnt in JavaScript does not follow all the syntax here But what JSX does is whenever JSX is written in the React form it internally converts to JavaScript It is transpiled to that code And that code has a special tool which transpiles which is called Babel Babel is a tool which transpiles JSX code which converts it to pure JavaScript So whenever we write JSX code in React First it is in JSX Then it is used as an internal tool which converts it to JavaScript And finally the rendering on the page or the logic of our page or whatever things are displayed on our browser All these are displayed using this JavaScript code Now in this, the beginner can hit the first question in our mind that when we had to display JavaScript on a browser So why did JSX write code which Babel has transpiled to and then converts it to JavaScript The simplest answer is that if we learn JSX once we will cover JSX in one or two classes So it is very easy to implement JSX When we start writing component-wise things it becomes more readable and it becomes easier to write as compared to normal JavaScript to render the same amount of UI components But the whole conversion of JSX to JavaScript internally which Babel does is behind the scenes It will never show us that it will react internally when we set up our computer So our internal tools will make this conversion We will not have to write any code separately We will not have to do anything separately If we want we can also go to the Babel page which is Babel.js.io If we go here and click on try it out then we will open some code like this Now here in the sandbox which we have seen the code of the app We will copy it and paste it here So this was our code of the React which we had written in JSX Babel internally changes it in this format and this format is our pure JavaScript All the things in this will show you JavaScript, Function, My Button What is returning Some call is being made in the name of JSX Button is passing through as a string Object is passing through In which children are written Again we have a function in the name of My App JSXS is returning a div is being created H1 element is being created in the children of div So this is how Babel works Babel is the pure JSX code which converts it into a normal JavaScript in some way But Babel will automatically do this conversion We don't need to partake in it So this was the basic of React How does React work and in React it is written in the code format Now we are finally going to set up the React on our local machine We will start creating a React project a basic app and with that we will slowly understand how we can build our components
